title: MCP-A Common Definitions
description: Shared $defs referenced by the MCP-A primitive request/response schemas. Reference a definition via, e.g., {"$ref": "https://mcp-a.dev/schemas/common.defs.json#/$defs/Citation"}.

Citation.title: Citation
Citation.description: A source reference supporting a compiled answer (SPEC §3 query response).

source_system.description: Underlying source system the cited data came from (e.g., 'salesforce-crm'). MUST be present on every citation.

domain_id.description: The information domain the citation belongs to (e.g., 'salesforce-crm').

entity_type.description: Entity type of the cited record (e.g., 'Account', 'Opportunity').

snippet.description: The relevant quote/excerpt from the source.

confidence.description: Per-citation confidence in [0.0, 1.0], present only when confidence was requested.

ClarificationField.title: ClarificationField
ClarificationField.description: A single piece of information the service still needs before it can execute an action (SPEC §action, clarification rounds). The field `name` is the key the client MUST use in the continuation request's `inputs` map.

name.description: Field key the client MUST supply in the continuation `inputs` map (e.g., 'due_date').

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this field is and why it is needed.

type.description: Expected value type for the supplied input.

required.description: Whether the service cannot proceed without this field. Defaults to true when omitted.

example.description: An example value to guide the client (any JSON type).

enum.description: Permitted values, when the field is constrained to a fixed set.

ActionEffect.title: ActionEffect
ActionEffect.description: A single state-changing effect the service applied at a source system while executing an action (SPEC §action response). Returned in `effects` when status is `completed`.

kind.description: The kind of state change applied.

source_system.description: Underlying source system where the effect was applied (e.g., 'salesforce-crm'). MUST be present on every effect.

entity_id.description: Identifier of the affected record, when one exists (e.g., the ID of a created task).

detail.description: Optional implementation-defined context about the effect.

Domain.title: Domain
Domain.description: An information domain entry returned by discover (SPEC §1).

description.description: What questions this domain can answer.

example_questions.description: Sample questions this domain can answer.

source_systems.description: Names of the underlying source systems backing this domain.

freshness_seconds.description: Age of the data in this domain, in seconds. MUST be returned so clients can decide whether to ask.

access_scope.description: Scope at which the domain is shared (e.g., 'user-scoped', 'team-scoped', 'org-scoped').

requires_context_fields.description: Context fields a query MUST supply to use this domain (e.g., ['account_id'] for a per-account domain).

natural_language_guidance.description: OPTIONAL query-building guidance (SPEC §1, MAEP-0005). Prose suggestions and example questions that help a client construct well-formed questions for this domain. Full-tier; a Core server omits it. SHOULD be auto-derived from the underlying backend's own metadata (GraphQL/OpenAPI descriptions, SQL comments) rather than hand-authored.

query_templates.description: OPTIONAL structured query patterns the domain prefers (SPEC §1, MAEP-0005). Each template is a human-readable pattern with `{variable}` placeholders and the enumerated choices for each. Lets a client construct queries by template instantiation instead of free-form text. Content grows freely; the field shape is fixed.

disambiguation_hints.description: OPTIONAL hints about fields that commonly require clarification (SPEC §1, MAEP-0005). Helps a client anticipate what the server may ask for in a query clarification round.

QueryTemplate.title: QueryTemplate
QueryTemplate.description: A structured query pattern advertised on a discover Domain entry (SPEC §1, MAEP-0005). `template` carries `{variable}` placeholders; `variables[]` enumerates the allowed choices for each placeholder.

template.description: Human-readable pattern with `{variable}` placeholders (e.g., 'revenue by {time_period} for {customer_type}').

variables.description: Enumerated choices for each `{variable}` placeholder in `template`.

name.description: Placeholder name, matching a `{name}` token in `template`.

values.description: Allowed values the client may substitute for this placeholder.

DisambiguationHint.title: DisambiguationHint
DisambiguationHint.description: A hint about a field that commonly needs clarification when querying a domain (SPEC §1, MAEP-0005).

description.description: What the field is and what happens if it is omitted (e.g., a default is applied, or a clarification round is triggered).

example.description: An example value for the field.

AccessScope.title: AccessScope
AccessScope.description: The set of resources the authenticated user is authorized to see (SPEC §5 context, §RBAC). RBAC-evaluated per-request, never cached. Additional dimensions are permitted.

resource_tags.description: Resource tags the user is permitted to see.

RoutingDecision.title: RoutingDecision
RoutingDecision.description: How and why an answer was routed (SPEC §6 explain.routing_decision; also referenced by query/follow_up responses when debug/explain requested).

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of why the chosen domains were selected.

alternative_routings.description: Routings considered but not chosen, with their relative scores.

score.description: Relative confidence of this alternative in [0.0, 1.0].

reason.description: Why this alternative was scored as it was.

reused_prior_routing.description: For follow_up: true when the prior query's routing was reused rather than re-routed (SPEC §4).

ResponseSchemaTarget.title: ResponseSchemaTarget
ResponseSchemaTarget.description: Tagged discriminator naming the target schema for a structured-response query (SPEC §3, RESOLVED beta). Clients MUST inspect `kind` to interpret `value`.

kind.description: Discriminator selecting how `value` is interpreted: a named schema, a domain's published ontology, or an inline schema definition.

value.description: For kind=schema_ref: a schema name (string). For kind=domain: a domain_id (string). For kind=inline: an inline schema definition (object).

Error.title: Error
Error.description: Abstract MCP-A error object (SPEC §Error Model). See error.json for the canonical standalone definition.
